title: AmazonSaleReport
description: Amazon India e-commerce orders, Q2 2022. One row = one order line.

index.description: Auto-generated row index. Not a meaningful business key.

Order ID.description: Unique identifier for each order. Use to count distinct orders or join tables.

Date.description: Date the order was placed (DD-MM-YY). Parse to datetime before any time-series work.

Status.description: Current fulfilment state of the order at time of data export.

Fulfilment.description: Who handled the physical dispatch — Amazon's own logistics or a third-party merchant.

Sales Channel.description: Platform through which the sale was made.

ship-service-level.description: Shipping speed tier chosen by the customer.

Style.description: Design or style code for the product. Groups variants of the same design.

SKU.description: Stock Keeping Unit — unique code per product+size+colour combination.

Category.description: Product category. Main dimension for product-level analysis.

Size.description: Garment size. Use for inventory and sizing demand analysis.

ASIN.description: Amazon Standard Identification Number — Amazon's own product ID. Can be used to look up listings.

Courier Status.description: Delivery outcome reported by the courier (e.g. Delivered, Undelivered, Returned to Seller).

Qty.description: Number of units ordered. Usually 1. Multiply by Amount for total value when Qty > 1.

currency.description: Currency code. Almost always INR — safe to treat as constant.

Amount.description: Order value in Indian Rupees (₹). Your primary revenue metric. May be null for cancelled orders.

ship-city.description: Destination city. Use for geographic demand mapping.

ship-state.description: Destination state (Indian state names). Best geographic grain for regional analysis.

ship-postal-code.description: 6-digit Indian PIN code. Finer-grained than state; useful for city-level mapping.

ship-country.description: Destination country. Constant (IN) — safe to ignore.

promotion-ids.description: Comma-separated list of promotion/discount codes applied. Null if no promotion used.

B2B.description: True if the buyer is a registered business customer (B2B). False for regular consumers (B2C).

fulfilled-by.description: More granular fulfilment detail. 'Easy Ship' = Amazon-managed last-mile. Null = Merchant-fulfilled.

notes.primary_key: Order ID (not always unique — one order can have multiple rows for multiple items)
notes.currency: All monetary values in INR (Indian Rupee)
notes.nulls: Amount is commonly null on Cancelled orders. promotion-ids is null when no deal applied.
